action_choice_hints: Whether the action cell is in the same row or column as a special token
Whether the action cell is the nearest cell to a special token along a cardinal direction
Whether there exists an uninterrupted path from the action cell to a boundary without encountering an obstacle
Whether the action cell is adjacent to a contiguous segment of special tokens
Whether the action cell is within K steps of a special token, for small K
Whether the action cell is at the end of a contiguous run of special tokens
Whether the action cell is in a region (e.g., top or bottom band) containing more special tokens
Whether all cells between the action cell and a boundary are empty
Whether the action cell is the first empty cell encountered along a direction from a special token
Whether the action cell is surrounded by empty cells in all four cardinal directions
Whether the action cell is part of a maximal contiguous segment of empty cells
Whether the action cell is blocked by an obstacle before reaching a special token along a direction
Whether the action cell is the only empty cell in its row or column
Whether the action cell is equidistant from multiple special tokens
Whether the action cell is at a corner or edge of the grid
Whether the action cell is in a row or column with the highest count of special tokens
transition_hints: Clicking an empty cell converts it into a special token
Only the clicked cell changes state; all other cells remain unchanged
If the clicked cell is already a special token, no change occurs
The environment does not move or remove any special tokens automatically
Obstacles, if present, are static and unaffected by actions
The set of special tokens can only increase or remain the same after each action
No cells revert from special token to empty as a result of any action
The grid size and boundaries remain fixed throughout transitions
Actions are deterministic: the same (s, a) always yields the same s_{t+1}
No new obstacles or special tokens appear except by explicit action
feature_family_hints: Directional scan features: needed to detect first empty or special token along a direction
Contiguity features: to capture whether a cell is part of a contiguous segment of empty or special tokens
Row/column aggregation features: to count special tokens or empty cells in a row or column
Adjacency features: to check if a cell is adjacent to a special token or obstacle
Path-clearance features: to determine if a path from a cell to a boundary is unobstructed
Region-based features: to compare token distributions in different grid regions (e.g., bands)
Edge/corner features: to identify if a cell is at the grid's edge or corner
Maximal segment features: to find if a cell is part of the largest contiguous run
Distance-to-token features: to compute distance from a cell to the nearest special token
Uniqueness features: to check if a cell is the only empty cell in its row or column
Equidistance features: to determine if a cell is equally distant from multiple special tokens
Blocked-path features: to detect if an obstacle blocks the path between a cell and a special token
Global count features: to summarize total counts of special tokens or empty cells
Local neighborhood features: to assess the configuration of cells in a small window around the action cell
Boundary-proximity features: to measure how close a cell is to the grid boundary

diversity_requirements: Include at least one template for each of: row/column relations, directional scans, and contiguity structure
Require templates that combine both local (adjacency) and global (row/column/region) context
placeholder_usage_limits: Limit the number of free coordinate placeholders per template to two
Restrict the maximum scan length or radius in directional features to a small constant (e.g., 4)
avoidances: Avoid templates that only check the raw token at the action cell without context
Avoid templates that encode absolute coordinates or grid positions
Avoid templates that duplicate the same relation with only minor parameter changes
